sourceMovie.title: What Dreams May Come
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto What Dreams May Come (1998) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Meet Joe Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What Dreams May Come clicked for you, Meet Joe Black (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after What Dreams May Come?
faq.answer: Try Meet Joe Black, A Ghost Story, City of Angels—each shares DNA with What Dreams May Come while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is What Dreams May Come?
faq.answer: What Dreams May Come (1998) is best described as Drama, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is What Dreams May Come worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
